claim: The choice between plain gallows t- and p- prefixes on Hand A paragraph-initial tokens is NOT random. It predicts one or more of: (A) folio section type (herbal, pharmaceutical, biological, recipes, etc.), (B) quire position (scribal-session structure, supports Timm continuous-evolution), or (C) within-folio position (liturgical/antiphon alternation pattern). If one or more predictors reach significance at locked thresholds, the gallows decoration encodes structured information at that axis. If none reach significance, t/p alternation is decorative noise on a closed-class header pool.
evidence_for: H-BV-GALLOWS-STRIP-HEADER-01 documented that the SAME base stem (chor, ol, shol, cho, cheol, aiin, odaiin) appears at paragraph-initial under BOTH t- and p- gallows — the decoration is systematically applied to a small base-stem pool

The t vs p choice is highly unbalanced in the recurring-stem data (e.g. tol 12 vs pol 1; tor 5 vs por 1; tchor 10 vs pchor 4 — strong t-preference with variable p-minority), suggesting the choice is structured rather than 50/50 random
Prior Brain-V work established plain gallows t/p as paragraph markers with different enrichment (2.8x and 5.0x) — they are already known to carry structural information

`H-BV-TIMM-CHALLENGE-01` produced a PARTIAL verdict showing quire-anchored effects on several markers; the t/p alternation is the natural candidate for the fine-grained quire-position signal Timm predicts
evidence_against: With 268 paragraphs and a t/p-unbalanced distribution, sample sizes per section may be small (e.g. <20 paragraphs per section for several sections), limiting chi-square power

Section tags in the corpus are image-derived (illustrations) not text-derived; if t/p encodes a TEXT-internal distinction unrelated to illustration, the section test returns null even if the alternation is meaningful
Quire information is coarse (ZL3b quire tags are single letters A-T); a quire-position test with <20 quires may be under-powered
Within-folio alternation depends on folios having >=3 gallows-paragraphs; many folios may have only 1-2 paragraphs
test:
LOCKED FOUR-PART PROCEDURE on Hand A.

  DATA:
    - Paragraphs: re-detect via H-BV-HIGH-LOW-STRUCTURE-01 / CIRCA-INSTANS-BENCHMARK-01 gallows rule.
    - Filter: keep paragraphs whose paragraph-initial token starts with plain gallows 't' or 'p' (first char t or p, second char not h if length >= 2). Drop paragraphs whose first token starts with cth/ckh/cph benched-gallows, or doesn't start with gallows at all (first-of-folio openers).
    - Per retained paragraph, record (gallows_letter, folio, quire, section, within_folio_index).

  BASELINE: global t-vs-p ratio. Report.

  TEST A — SECTION PREDICTOR:
    Chi-square on 2 x K contingency table (t vs p) x (section) where K = number of sections with >=10 retained paragraphs.
    PASS if p_value < 0.01 AND Cramer's V >= 0.20 (medium-to-large effect).

  TEST B — QUIRE PREDICTOR:
    Chi-square on 2 x Q contingency table (t vs p) x (quire) where Q = number of quires with >=10 retained paragraphs.
    PASS if p_value < 0.01 AND Cramer's V >= 0.20.

  TEST C — WITHIN-FOLIO ALTERNATION:
    For folios with >=3 retained paragraphs, compute the per-folio sequence of t/p letters.
    Metric: mean runs test z-score (Wald-Wolfowitz runs test) aggregated across folios with >=3 paragraphs.
    Observed number of runs vs expected under random binomial with folio's t-rate.
    PASS if the cross-folio aggregate |z| >= 2.58 (two-sided p < 0.01) in EITHER direction:
      positive z -> alternation (liturgical-style t,p,t,p)
      negative z -> clustering (block structure t,t,t,p,p,p)

  TEST D — STEM-CONDITIONAL ALTERNATION:
    For the top-10 recurring stripped stems, compute P(t | stem) and test whether this differs SIGNIFICANTLY by stem (chi-square on 2 x 10 table). This tests whether some stems prefer t while others prefer p — a stem-level constraint on the decoration.
    PASS if p_value < 0.01 AND Cramer's V >= 0.20.
test_threshold:
Report the verdict for each of tests A/B/C/D independently. Overall interpretation:
  - If A passes: Hand A encodes a binary content attribute at section level.
  - If B passes (and A doesn't): Hand A shows scribal-session structure (Timm-compatible).
  - If C passes: Hand A has liturgical/antiphon-style within-folio alternation.
  - If D passes: t/p is stem-linked (lexical-phonological constraint rather than positional structure).
  - If 0 of 4 pass: t/p is decorative noise; the 14-item header stem pool is the primary finding.
  Multiple-test caveat: with 4 tests at p<0.01, family-wise error ~0.04; passes near threshold should be interpreted cautiously. A single clean pass (p<0.001 AND V>=0.30) is more decisive than a p-in-0.005-0.01 pass.

tests_run.details:
0 of 4 tests pass the locked threshold (p<0.01 AND V>=0.20, or |z|>=2.58 for runs test). Retained 216 of 268 paragraphs (those starting with plain gallows t or p, excluding benched-gallows cth/ckh/cph and non-gallows first-of-folio openers). Global t-rate: 135/216 = 62.5%.

  Test A section: herbal 58.2% t (158), recipes 84.2% t (19), pharmaceutical 69.2% t (39). chi2=5.805, df=2, p=0.0549, V=0.164. NEAR-MISS at p=0.055; recipes section shows striking t-bias.

  Test B quire: quires G/H/O/Q cluster at 71-84% t; quires A/B/C/E/F at 47-60% t. chi2=13.218, df=9, p=0.153, V=0.251. Cramer's V EXCEEDS threshold (0.251 > 0.20) but chi-square not significant because df=9 dilutes across many quires.

  Test C within-folio runs: Stouffer combined z=-2.014 across 23 folios with >=3 gallows-paragraphs. Negative z indicates CLUSTERING rather than alternation. |z|=2.014 misses the 2.58 threshold but is marginally significant at two-tailed p=0.044.

  Test D stem-conditional: top-10 stems show wide t-rate variation (ol 92% t, cheol 20% t, or 83% t, chor 71% t). chi2=10.855, df=9, p=0.286, V=0.406. LARGE effect size (V=0.406) but chi-square not significant due to df=9 dilution.

  Verdict: per locked rule, 0 passes -> DECORATIVE NOISE interpretation. BUT — tests B (V=0.25) and D (V=0.41) show medium-to-large effect sizes that fail only on the chi-square p-value because of degrees-of-freedom dilution. Tests A and C show directional near-misses at p=0.05-0.04. The 216-paragraph sample is spread thin across 10 quires / 3 sections / 10 stems, limiting statistical power. The locked conclusion is noise, but the effect-size pattern suggests UNDER-POWERED rather than ABSENT signal.

verdict_note: Locked rule returns NOISE (0 of 4 significance-tests pass). The 14-item header stem pool from GALLOWS-STRIP-HEADER-01 remains the primary finding. HOWEVER the near-miss pattern is worth flagging: Test B (quire) and Test D (stem) both have medium-to-large Cramer's V effect sizes (0.25 and 0.41) that fail the chi-square p-test because df=9 dilutes the statistic. Test A (section) near-misses at p=0.055 with recipes section showing 84% t-bias. Test C shows directional clustering (z=-2.014) at borderline significance. A better-powered follow-up (pairwise contrasts rather than omnibus chi-square, or larger paragraph set including benched-gallows initials) might reveal a real signal that this omnibus test missed. For the locked verdict: t/p is not a diagnostic structural axis in these data under these tests.
findings_for_followup: Pairwise chi-square contrasts between specific quire clusters (G/H/O/Q high-t vs A/B/C/E/F mid-t) would concentrate the signal from the diluted Test B. If this specific contrast reaches p<0.001 with large effect, it's a meaningful quire-cluster structure that the omnibus missed.

Stem 'cheol' is the single clear p-preferring stem (20% t vs the 62.5% global t-rate). This is 4 of 5 'cheol' occurrences under p-gallows. Investigate whether 'cheol' instances are content-distinct from 'chor'/'chol'/'cho' instances on the surrounding folios — the stem-specific p-preference may carry semantic load even if omnibus Test D didn't reach significance.

Within-folio clustering (Test C z=-2.014) suggests PARAGRAPHS GROUP BY GALLOWS within a folio (t-block then p-block or vice versa). Test this directly with a BLOCK-SEGMENTATION test: does each folio split cleanly into a t-majority and p-majority section? If so, it's a sub-folio structural unit smaller than section but larger than paragraph.

The 216-paragraph sample could be extended by INCLUDING benched-gallows-initial paragraphs (cth/ckh/cph at paragraph-initial). These were excluded from the current test. If the ternary distinction t/p/benched encodes structure, including them may sharpen the signal.
reasoning: GALLOWS-STRIP-HEADER-01 established that ~14 stems appear at paragraph-initial under both t- and p- gallows. The alternation is the single most diagnostic feature Brain-V can test cheaply with existing data. Four pre-registered hypotheses span the space of possible information the t/p choice could encode: content (section), scribal (quire), positional (within-folio), lexical (stem). Running all four gives a clean map of which axis (if any) carries signal.
source: Brain-V 2026-04-16 user-directed test following GALLOWS-STRIP-HEADER-01 REFUTED result; user flagged t/p alternation as the highest-diagnostic next test
